title: poa nemoralis subsp. uniflora
intro: Poa nemoralis subsp. uniflora, also known as the solitary meadow-grass, is a subspecies of the tufted hair-grass, a type of perennial grass native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and antiseptic properties, which have been employed to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Poa nemoralis subsp. uniflora include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb in the treatment of various conditions. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and stems, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or topical applications for medicinal purposes.
